summary.intent: Perform a pre-landing code review of CHA-15 (Wire CRM into gateway), which adds automatic contact creation and interaction logging to the channel event pipeline by hooking CRM operations into GatewayChannelEventSink::emit().
summary.outcome: Review completed with 1 informational fix applied (dead `args` tuple and `drop(args)` call removed from a test). No critical issues found; the diff was approved as clean.

repo: CRM subsystem is fully built at storage/RPC layers (CrmStore trait, SqliteCrmStore, MemoryCrmStore, LiveCrmService, 16 RPC methods) but had zero channel event integration before this change.

GatewayChannelEventSink uses Arc<tokio::sync::OnceCell<Arc<GatewayState>>> for deferred state access; CRM store is passed directly at construction via a with_crm builder method rather than going through GatewayState.
CRM store is accessed directly (bypassing CrmService/JSON round-trip) for internal gateway operations — established pattern from the retention engine.
All CRM writes are tokio::spawn fire-and-forget to never block message delivery; failures log warn! and return silently.
channel_webhook_dedup.rs handles HTTP-level dedup; CRM interaction logging is downstream and accepts duplicates by design.
Feature is gated both by #[cfg(feature = "crm")] compile-time flag and config.crm.enabled runtime flag.
Contact name update uses UPSERT with a benign TOCTOU — worst case a concurrent name update is lost, which is acceptable for display names.
No TODOS.md exists in the repo.

code.finding: Dead code: `args` tuple was constructed and only passed to `drop(args)` in the `logs_multiple_interactions_for_same_contact` test. Removed the dead tuple and drop call.

workflow: The review used a two-pass structure: Pass 1 for critical issues (SQL injection, race conditions, LLM trust boundaries, enum completeness) and Pass 2 for informational/style issues.
Greptile comment fetch is part of the review preamble; when no comments exist the review proceeds without them.
Auto-fixes during review (like the dead code removal) are applied inline and reported in the review summary rather than opened as separate tickets.

friction: No Greptile comments were available, so the review relied entirely on manual diff inspection without automated triage hints.
open_items: Contact merge/dedup — same person on multiple channels creates separate contacts (future ticket needed).
Outbound interaction logging — only inbound messages create interactions; outbound/bot responses are not logged.
CRM Web UI — contact timeline view in the web dashboard.
GDPR documentation — auto-contact creation from inbound messages has privacy implications that need documentation.
Interaction deduplication — currently accepts duplicates from webhook retries; retention engine handles growth.
Metrics behind #[cfg(feature = "metrics")] (crm.contacts.auto_created, crm.interactions.logged, crm.errors) are specified but implementation not confirmed in transcript.
CRM docs do not yet exist — no staleness concern now but will need to be created as the feature matures.
